$comment: Reference case for buoyancy (F = fluidDensity * submergedVolume * g). Single free coefficient (g); the two inputs are swept over realistic water + volume ranges. Linear in g, so this is the easiest possible three-factor product fit — a cheap sanity check that the LM pipeline does not regress on a trivial case.
notes: fluidDensity sweeps freshwater (997) and seawater (1025); submergedVolume from ≈half-litre to 1 m³. F scales to ~10 kN at the high end; rmse_max=0.5 N is strict relative to the signal. relative_tolerance 1 % on g is realistic for a linear fit.
